summary: Verified 2026 Medicare facts used to ground generated scripts so they are accurate and compliant.

facts.fact: The 2026 Annual Enrollment Period runs October 15 to December 7.
In 2026 there is a hard $2,100 annual cap on out-of-pocket prescription costs; after that the plan covers 100% of covered drugs for the rest of the year.
The Medicare Prescription Payment Plan (M3P) lets members spread out-of-pocket drug costs into smaller monthly payments, and now auto-renews.
The maximum Part D deductible rose to $615 in 2026 (up from $590 in 2025).
Insulin stays capped at $35/month (or lower) in 2026.
Starting Jan 1, 2026, Medicare uses newly negotiated lower prices on the first group of selected high-cost drugs.
About 75% of Medicare Advantage drug-plan enrollees pay no premium beyond the Part B premium in 2026; average supplemental premium ~$15/month.
Most MA plans still include vision, dental and hearing; OTC, meal, transportation and flex allowances are more limited in 2026 than 2025.
For 2026 the VBID model ended; grocery/flex allowances are now offered only under SSBCI to enrollees with qualifying chronic conditions. Do NOT promise a grocery/cash card to everyone.
A flex/spending card is a plan-specific allowance for items like food, OTC or utilities; amounts and rules vary by plan, and many expire monthly.

compliance_rules: Never imply you are from Medicare, CMS, or a government agency. State you are a licensed insurance agency / agent.
Do not claim a benefit is 'pending', 'expiring', or 'will go unclaimed' to create false urgency.
Do not promise a specific dollar grocery/cash card unless the person qualifies under SSBCI (chronic condition) and a plan in their area offers it.
Honor Do-Not-Call immediately; identify yourself and the company up front (CMS marketing rules).
Make only accurate statements; confirm Part A & B to assess eligibility, not as a scare tactic.
